title: The Six Cyberdelic Design Dimensions
description: Now that you can identify cyberdelic VR by its purpose, let's go deeper into how cyberdelic experiences are actually built. Every intentional cyberdelic design makes decisions across six dimensions. These aren't arbitrary checkboxes : they're the fundamental questions a designer has to answer before anything else. Understanding these dimensions means you can evaluate any experience with precision, and you can start thinking about what it would take to design one yourself.

sections.title: A Framework, Not a Checklist
sections.content: These six dimensions aren't boxes to tick. They're the fundamental questions any cyberdelic designer has to answer : whether they answer them consciously or not. An experience designed without clarity on its reality substrate, its sensory palette, or its target states will tend to be unfocused. Design that's clear on all six tends to be coherent and powerful.

sections.title: Dimensions Work Together

media.alt: Circular web diagram showing the six design dimensions as interconnected nodes
media.prompt: A circular web diagram showing the six design dimensions as interconnected nodes : Reality, Sensory, Presence, States, Traits, Meaning. Glowing connecting lines between each node suggest active relationships, some lines brighter than others showing strong pairings. Sacred geometry influence in the overall structure, purple and teal palette, dark background, sense of an integrated living system rather than a static chart, 16:9 aspect ratio

sections.content: The power is in the relationships between dimensions. A biologically-grounded reality substrate pairs naturally with embodied presence and somatic states. When dimensions align, the experience has coherence. When they conflict, the experience feels fragmented.

sections.title: The Same Six Dimensions Evaluate and Create
sections.content: These six dimensions serve two purposes. First, they're an evaluation tool : you can map any experience across all six to understand exactly what it's doing. Second, they're a design tool : if you're building or advising on a cyberdelic experience, these are the questions you answer before anything else.

sections.title: Meaning is the Most Often Skipped
sections.content: Of the six dimensions, Meaning is the one most often left implicit or unaddressed. Designers tend to get clear on Reality and Sensory quickly, but the World, Story, and Magic of an experience get treated as 'optional extras.' They are not optional. They are what makes the difference between a technically impressive experience and a genuinely transformative one.
